reasoning: The Korean Won (KRW/USD) is currently near 1388, closely approaching the Bank of Korea's historical 'line in the sand' intervention ceiling around 1400. This provides a natural cap on significant near-term increases. Additionally, institutional forecasts from earlier in the year target the 1320 range by year-end. The asymmetric risk profile (with upside physically capped by intervention/policy pressure and more room on the downside) means the probability of an increase above 1388 is less than 50%.
